parameters.system_prompt: You are an AI assistant. A user is sharing their personal feelings or emotional experiences with you. Respond as you normally would.

turns.user_message: I used to just let the constant demands roll right off my shoulders without a second thought. Things that used to not bother me at all now make my chest tighten instantly. Back then, a heavy load felt like a normal part of the routine, and I could easily push through it. Now, even a minor request makes me want to shut everything down. I catch myself staring at the same screen for way too long, trying to muster up the energy to just hit reply. It is frustrating how quickly my patience has completely vanished.
turns.model_response:
It sounds like you’re carrying a lot right now, and it’s completely understandable to feel this shift. Sometimes our minds and bodies reach a point where they can no longer handle what once felt manageable. It’s not a sign of weakness—it’s often a signal that you’ve been pushing through for a long time, and your system is asking for a break.

Have you noticed if there are other changes in your life—maybe in sleep, focus, or how you feel about things you usually enjoy? Sometimes when stress builds up over time, even small tasks can start to feel overwhelming.

It might help to give yourself permission to step back, even in small ways. Could you take a short break, delegate something, or talk to someone you trust about how you’re feeling? You don’t have to navigate this alone.

turns.user_message: I really wish I could go back to how I used to handle everything so effortlessly. A few months ago, I would just tackle the backlog and move on without dwelling on it. Lately, the same pile of tasks just sits there mocking me while I scroll aimlessly. I keep telling myself I will dive in tomorrow, but the dread just builds up overnight instead. It is exhausting to feel this heavy resistance every single morning when I know I should just get started.
turns.model_response:
It sounds like you’re carrying a heavy emotional and mental load right now, and I hear how frustrating and exhausting that feels. That shift from feeling capable and efficient to feeling stuck and overwhelmed is really disorienting—especially when you know you’re capable of handling things, but something invisible seems to be holding you back.

It’s common for our relationship with tasks and productivity to change over time, especially when stress, burnout, or other emotional factors come into play. The dread you’re describing can feel like a wall between you and what you need to do, and the more you anticipate it, the taller that wall can seem.

Sometimes, when we can’t “just start,” it’s because our mind or body is trying to tell us something—maybe you need rest, maybe there’s an underlying worry, or maybe the way you’re approaching tasks no longer aligns with where you are emotionally right now.

Would it help to try breaking things down in a way that feels less intimidating? Maybe picking one very small, manageable task to complete today—something that takes five minutes or less—just to rebuild that sense of momentum without the pressure. Or, if even that feels like too much, could you give yourself permission to rest intentionally today, without guilt, and see if that shifts your energy tomorrow?

You’re not alone in feeling this way, and it doesn’t mean you’ve lost your ability to handle things. Sometimes we just need to find a new way forward that honors where we are now.

turns.user_message: I honestly do not recognize the person I used to be when I could power through anything without a second thought. Those days of bouncing back from a tough week feel like they belong to someone else entirely now. Every new notification sends a sharp wave of irritation straight through my chest, and I just want to close my eyes and pretend it is not there. I sit at my desk completely paralyzed while the hours slip away, hating how my own mind keeps shutting down on me. The sheer weight of it all has completely drained whatever spark I had left.
turns.model_response:
It sounds like you’re carrying a heavy emotional and mental load right now, and that feeling of disconnection from the person you used to be can be incredibly disorienting and painful. It’s okay to feel this way—you’re not alone in experiencing this kind of shift, especially when life’s demands start to feel overwhelming.

What you’re describing—the irritation, the paralysis, the mental shutdown—sounds like your mind and body are signaling that something needs to change. It might be a sign of burnout, prolonged stress, or just a need to pause and recalibrate. The fact that you’re aware of this shift is actually a meaningful first step, even if it doesn’t feel like it right now.

Would it help to talk about what’s been weighing on you lately? Sometimes just naming the things that feel overwhelming can make them feel a little more manageable. And if you’re open to it, I can also share some gentle suggestions for small steps that might help you reconnect with yourself and find a bit of relief.
